The report interprets this as a script-level abort or return code from a failed transfer utility.'}, {'id': 'symptom_aborted_reason', 'name': 'Declared failure reason Aborted', 'description': 'CloudBees executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.'}, {'id': 'symptom_pre_step_io_contention', 'name': 'Pre-step IO contention', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed system.io.await peaking at 15.97ms and system.io.util at 15.97% around 2026-07-07 06:19:23 UTC, about 27 seconds before VerifySrc started.'}, {'id': 'symptom_massive_outbound_burst', 'name': 'Massive outbound network burst', 'description': 'Datadog system.net.bytes_sent showed about 354MB outbound during the step window in bursts of approximately 55MB, 40MB, 54MB, 107MB, and 98MB.'}, {'id': 'symptom_minimal_inbound_traffic', 'name': 'Minimal inbound traffic', 'description': 'During the step, inbound traffic was only about 1.2–1.5MB, with no large source-fetch download pattern observed.'}, {'id': 'symptom_rising_system_load', 'name': 'Rising system load', 'description': 'Datadog system.load.1 rose from 0.81 to 1.97 across the correlation window, consistent with accumulating concurrent process pressure.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_user_drop', 'name': 'Abrupt CPU user drop', 'description': 'Datadog system.cpu.user dropped from about 1.02% to 0.07% near the end of the step, consistent with process termination.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_outbound_transfer_timeout_under_io_pressure', 'name': 'Probable outbound transfer timeout or abort under pre-existing IO pressure', 'description': 'The report identifies the probable root cause as the VerifySrc step initiating a large outbound transfer of about 354MB while the agent storage subsystem was already under IO contention from a concurrent workload. The upload may have stalled, timed out, or been rejected/dropped by the remote endpoint; VerifySrc then exited with code 2 and CloudBees classified the step as Aborted.'}]

Last recorded occurrence was 07:29:59Z.'}, {'id': 'sym_cifs_active_before_window', 'name': 'CIFS errors active at query window start', 'description': 'CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors were already firing at 556/min at 06:00:00Z, indicating the outage began before the query window.'}, {'id': 'sym_kerberos_key_expired', 'name': 'Kerberos key expired events', 'description': "214 explicit '[Errno 127] Key has expired' events were observed across 24 build hosts between 06:18:40Z and 06:55:27Z on QCDFS mount paths."}, {'id': 'sym_qcdfs_inaccessible', 'name': 'QCDFS inaccessible to build hosts', 'description': 'QCDFS.qualcomm.com CIFS/SMB shared file service mounted at /mnt/qcdfs/ was inaccessible because CIFS session setup authentication could not complete.'}, {'id': 'sym_verifysrc_window_cifs_errors', 'name': 'CIFS errors during VerifySrc window', 'description': 'During the 06:27Z–06:31Z VerifySrc window, 2,126 CIFS errors were observed across 13 hosts at 564–570 errors per minute.'}, {'id': 'sym_exit_code_zero_masking', 'name': 'VerifySrc exited with code 0 despite failure', 'description': 'The VerifySrc process exited cleanly with code 0; failure was detected by verification logic, masking the infrastructure failure as a Compile failure.'}, {'id': 'sym_related_jobs_same_pattern', 'name': 'Three related jobs failed with same pattern', 'description': 'At minimum three CRMBuilds jobs failed in the same window with identical characteristics: f179cc92, f179cca2, and f179cd8b.'}, {'id': 'sym_no_formal_incident', 'name': 'No Datadog incident declared', 'description': 'No formal Datadog incident was declared for QCDFS, Kerberos, or CRM during the outage window despite the scale and duration.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'rc_farm_wide_kerberos_ticket_expiry', 'name': 'Farm-wide Kerberos ticket expiry causing QCDFS CIFS authentication failure', 'description': "Expired Kerberos credentials prevented CIFS/SMB session setup to \\\\QCDFS.qualcomm.com, causing source files on /mnt/qcdfs/ to be inaccessible during VerifySrc. The mechanism is supported by 214 '[Errno 127] Key has expired' events across 24 hosts and 50,676 CIFS SessSetup = -13 errors across 19 hosts."}]
    
 Data Gaps:  [{'id': 'gap_no_direct_crm_ubuntulv39_telemetry', 'name': 'No direct telemetry from crm-ubuntulv39', 'description': 'The affected agent host crm-ubuntulv39 has no Datadog telemetry coverage, so root cause cannot be confirmed from direct monitoring data for this specific host.'}, {'id': 'gap_commander_host_not_identified', 'name': 'Commander host not identified', 'description': 'The Commander host was not identified in the RCA; no evidence links Commander to the failure.'}, {'id': 'gap_exact_outage_start_unknown', 'name': 'Exact outage start time unknown', 'description': 'CIFS errors were already present at full rate at 06:00Z, so the outage began before the query window; the exact start time is not established.'}, {'id': 'gap_precise_kerberos_lifecycle_trigger_unknown', 'name': 'Precise Kerberos lifecycle trigger unknown', 'description': 'The RCA lists possible causes for the Kerberos expiry condition, including unrenewed TGTs, KDC unreachability for renewal, or uncoordinated keytab rotation, but does not establish which occurred.'}, {'id': 'gap_splunk_not_queried', 'name': 'Splunk not queried', 'description': 'Splunk was not queried; the RCA states Datadog flex logs provided conclusive evidence.'}, {'id': 'gap_complete_impacted_job_set_unknown', 'name': 'Complete impacted job set unknown', 'description': 'At least three CRMBuilds jobs were confirmed affected, but all other CRMBuilds jobs running during 06:00Z–07:30Z still need to be audited.'}, {'id': 'gap_direct_cloudbees_agent_logs_needed', 'name': 'Direct CloudBees step logs from crm-ubuntulv39 need examination', 'description': 'Because crm-ubuntulv39 lacks telemetry, the RCA recommends examining CloudBees step execution logs from that host for definitive host-local confirmation.'}]

Error message: Property '/myJob/SourceViolationRequired' does not exist."}, {'id': 'symptom_004', 'name': 'Make step aborted', 'description': 'Make step completed at the resource scheduler level but carried code=ABORTED and abortStatus=ABORT with errorHandling=abortJob.'}, {'id': 'symptom_005', 'name': 'VerifySrc step aborted', 'description': 'VerifySrc step failed with code=ABORTED and abortStatus=ABORT after the missing property error, with errorHandling=abortJob.'}, {'id': 'symptom_006', 'name': 'SaveBuild cascaded abort', 'description': 'SaveBuild failed after the abort chain was active. Job errorMessage stated that step SaveBuild failed and its error handling aborted the job.'}, {'id': 'symptom_007', 'name': 'AbortJobOperation sequence', 'description': 'AbortJobOperation events occurred on las-colo12-j13-10 at 04:04:38, 04:04:41, and 04:04:59 PDT.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'rc_001', 'name': 'Compile failure in Make step', 'description': "Primary root cause. The Make step, which executed common/build/build.sh for Hawi.LA.1.0, failed. The job's BuildInfoJson explicitly recorded status=Failed and failedReason=Compile."}, {'id': 'rc_002', 'name': 'Missing SourceViolationRequired job property', 'description': 'Secondary contributing cause. VerifySrc attempted to read /myJob/SourceViolationRequired, but the property did not exist on the job. Because VerifySrc used errorHandling=abortJob, the missing property independently triggered the abort chain.'}]
    
 Data Gaps:  [{'id': 'gap_001', 'name': 'Make step execution log not retrieved', 'description': 'The specific compiler error, failing source file, line number, and component were not available because the Make step execution log Make.f1792a35-bafc-f101-985d-1ea035bf3243.log was not retrieved.'}, {'id': 'gap_002', 'name': 'Unknown source of missing SourceViolationRequired property', 'description': 'The RCA log does not identify which upstream step or procedure definition was responsible for setting /myJob/SourceViolationRequired, nor whether that upstream action failed silently, was skipped, or was not defined for this build type.'}, {'id': 'gap_003', 'name': 'ARM license contribution not evidenced', 'description': 'The Setup command contained a comment about ARMLM_ONDEMAND_ACTIVATION, but the RCA log provides no direct evidence that ARM license acquisition contributed to the compile failure.'}]
log_file_b:
Log symptoms:  [{'id': 'symptom_exit_code_2', 'name': 'Exit Code 2', 'description': 'CloudBees step metadata reported failedStep.exitCode = "2" for VerifySrc, interpreted in the RCA as a script-level abort, timeout, or transfer failure.'}, {'id': 'symptom_aborted_failure_reason', 'name': 'Aborted Failure Reason', 'description': 'CloudBees execution context reported executionContext.failedReason = "Aborted", indicating the step terminated before natural completion.'}, {'id': 'symptom_io_await_peak_70_67ms', 'name': 'IO Await Peak 70.67ms', 'description': 'Datadog metrics for crm-ubuntu221 showed system.io.await = 70.67ms at approximately 06:07:40Z, the highest IO await across the analyzed session.'}, {'id': 'symptom_sustained_io_await_prestep', 'name': 'Sustained Pre-Step IO Await', 'description': 'IO await remained elevated before VerifySrc started, reported as 66.80ms, 31.16ms, 13.93ms, and 10.65ms from roughly 06:07:40Z to 06:09:14Z.'}, {'id': 'symptom_io_await_during_step', 'name': 'Continuous IO Await During VerifySrc', 'description': 'During VerifySrc, IO await reportedly never dropped below 11.24ms and reached 35.53ms, 28.23ms, and 24.78ms during the step window.'}, {'id': 'symptom_io_util_27_32_prestep', 'name': 'Pre-Step IO Utilization 27–32%', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed IO utilization of 27.47–32.20% from approximately 06:07:40–06:08:40Z.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_iowait_peak_3_55', 'name': 'CPU iowait Peak 3.55%', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed CPU iowait peaking at 3.55%, matching the period of the IO await spike.'}, {'id': 'symptom_inbound_burst_97mb', 'name': 'Inbound Burst About 97MB in 8 Seconds', 'description': 'Datadog network metrics showed about 48MB plus 49MB received in 8 seconds around 06:07:04–06:07:16Z.'}, {'id': 'symptom_outbound_555mb', 'name': 'Outbound Transfer About 555MB', 'description': 'Datadog network metrics showed sustained outbound transfer totaling approximately 555MB, the largest outbound volume across the analyzed jobs.'}, {'id': 'symptom_chronic_load_2_78_3_87', 'name': 'Chronic Host Load 2.78–3.87', 'description': 'Datadog system.load.1 remained between 2.78 and 3.87 throughout the observation window, never dropping below 2.78.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cross_job_VerifySrc_abort_pattern', 'name': 'Cross-Job VerifySrc Abort Pattern', 'description': 'The RCA identifies this as the fourth WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 across the session, spanning three agents and two fleets.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_extreme_io_contention', 'name': 'Extreme IO Contention Caused VerifySrc Abort', 'description': 'Primary probable root cause: a massive pre-step inbound burst of about 97MB in 8 seconds overwhelmed crm-ubuntu221 storage, causing IO await to spike to 70.67ms and remain elevated through the VerifySrc step; the VerifySrc upload operation then stalled under IO contention, exceeded a timeout threshold, and was aborted with exit code 2.'}, {'id': 'root_cause_chronic_host_overload_contributing', 'name': 'Chronic Host Overload Contributed', 'description': 'Contributing factor: load average stayed between 2.78 and 3.87 for the observation window, indicating concurrent workloads competing for IO and CPU resources and preventing storage recovery.'}, {'id': 'root_cause_systemic_VerifySrc_pattern', 'name': 'Systemic WLAN TFH VerifySrc Abort Pattern', 'description': 'The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.'}]
    
 Data Gaps:  [{'id': 'data_gap_step_stdout_stderr_missing', 'name': 'VerifySrc Step Logs Missing', 'description': 'Application-level VerifySrc stdout/stderr was not available in Datadog and must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.'}, {'id': 'data_gap_no_datadog_logs_crm_ubuntu221', 'name': 'No Application Logs Forwarded From crm-ubuntu221', 'description': 'Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for crm-ubuntu221.'}, {'id': 'data_gap_commander_host_unidentified', 'name': 'Commander Host Not Identified', 'description': 'The RCA states the Commander host was not identified and no evidence was available for Commander contribution.'}, {'id': 'data_gap_remote_upload_endpoint_unknown', 'name': 'Remote Upload Endpoint Unknown', 'description': 'The sustained outbound transfer of approximately 555MB went to an unknown remote upload endpoint; destination and endpoint behavior were not confirmed.'}, {'id': 'data_gap_specific_abort_trigger_unconfirmed', 'name': 'Specific Abort Trigger Unconfirmed', 'description': 'The RCA assigns medium confidence to the specific abort trigger because step logs are required to confirm whether IO timeout, upload failure, or script-level detection caused the abort.'}, {'id': 'data_gap_remote_endpoint_contribution_unconfirmed', 'name': 'Remote Endpoint Contribution Unconfirmed', 'description': 'The RCA says the remote upload endpoint may have been slow or unavailable, but this cannot be confirmed without step logs or endpoint evidence.'}]

'symptom_disk_usage_jump_secondary', 'name': 'Disk usage jump after this job', 'description': "Datadog metrics show disk usage on san-w175-ha1-4-07 was 5.13% at this job's step time and 42.1% during later job f179cb72, a reported increase of approximately 37% in about 8 minutes. The RCA treats this as a secondary risk, not the cause of this job's failure."}, {'id': 'symptom_overlapping_job_execution', 'name': 'Overlapping execution on same agent', 'description': 'Job f179cb72 reportedly started on san-w175-ha1-4-07 at 06:16:52Z while this job was still in teardown and before this job ended at 06:18:16Z.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_systemic_verifysrc_upload_abort', 'name': 'Probable systemic VerifySrc upload abort: remote endpoint failure or script timeout', 'description': 'The RCA identifies the probable root cause as a systemic VerifySrc upload operation issue. A sustained outbound transfer of approximately 74MB began before VerifySrc formally started and continued during the step, after which VerifySrc aborted with exit code 2. The same pattern across multiple WLAN TFH jobs and two agents points to either an unavailable or slow remote upload endpoint, or a VerifySrc script timeout that is too short for the data volume. The exact trigger is not confirmed without step logs.'}]

 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_farm_wide_Kerberos_ticket_expiry_QCDFS_auth_failure', 'name': 'Farm-wide Kerberos ticket expiry causing QCDFS CIFS authentication failure', 'description': 'The VerifySrc step failed because the build agent could not authenticate to \\\\QCDFS.qualcomm.com over CIFS/SMB to access source files.  Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_probable_code_defect', 'name': 'Probable CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317 code defect', 'description': 'The report assesses a code defect in the CQ2390.LA.1.0-00317 codebase as the most likely explanation because two different build variants of the same codebase snapshot failed simultaneously on different agents with identical exit code and failure reason.  Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_io_contention_network_io_storm', 'name': 'IO contention during compilation caused by network-IO storm', 'description': 'The RCA identifies the probable root cause as severe IO contention during the Make step. Large concurrent network transfers, approximately 91MB inbound and approximately 414MB outbound, competed with compiler reads and object-file writes, causing IO await to reach 10.84ms, IO utilization 17.2%, CPU iowait 3.63%, and system load 4.74 before Make exited with code 4.'}]

abort, timeout, or transfer failure.'}, {'id': 'symptom_aborted_failure_reason', 'name': 'Aborted Failure Reason', 'description': 'CloudBees execution context reported executionContext.failedReason = showed system.io.await = 70.67ms at approximately 06:07:40Z, the highest IO await across the analyzed session.'}, {'id': 'symptom_sustained_io_await_prestep', 'name': 'Sustained Pre-Step IO Await', 'symptom_cpu_iowait_peak_3_55', 'name': 'CPU iowait Peak 3.55%', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed CPU iowait peaking at 3.55%, matching the period of the IO await spike.'}, {'id': 'symptom_inbound_burst_97mb', 'name': 'Inbound Burst About 97MB in 8 Seconds', 'description': 'Datadog network metrics showed about 48MB plus 49MB received in 8 seconds around 06:07:04–06:07:16Z.'}, {'id': 'symptom_outbound_555mb', 'name': 'Outbound Transfer About 555MB', 'description': 'Datadog network metrics showed sustained outbound transfer totaling approximately 555MB, the largest outbound volume across the analyzed jobs.'}, {'id': 'symptom_chronic_load_2_78_3_87', 'name': 'Chronic Host Load 2.78–3.87', 'description': 'Datadog system.load.1 remained between 2.78 and 3.87 throughout the WLAN TFH VerifySrc abort with exit code 2 across the session, spanning three agents and two fleets.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_extreme_io_contention', 'name': 'Extreme IO Contention Caused VerifySrc Abort', 'description': 'Primary probable root cause: a massive pre-step inbound burst of about 97MB in 8 seconds overwhelmed crm-ubuntu221 storage, causing IO await to spike to 70.67ms and remain elevated through the VerifySrc step; the VerifySrc upload operation then stalled under IO contention, exceeded a timeout threshold, and was aborted with exit code 2.'}, {'id': 'root_cause_chronic_host_overload_contributing', 'name': 'Chronic Host Overload Contributed', 'description': 'Contributing factor: load average stayed between 2.78 and 3.87 for the observation window, indicating concurrent workloads competing for IO and CPU resources and preventing storage recovery.'}, {'id': 'root_cause_systemic_VerifySrc_pattern', 'name': 'Systemic WLAN TFH VerifySrc Abort Pattern', 'description': 'The RCA reports a systemic pattern across four WLAN TFH VerifySrc jobs with exit code 2 and Aborted classification across three agents and two fleets, suggesting VerifySrc script or remote endpoint involvement beyond a single agent.'}]

 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_outbound_transfer_timeout_under_io_pressure', 'name': 'Probable outbound transfer timeout or abort under pre-existing IO pressure', 'description': 'The report identifies the probable root cause as the VerifySrc step initiating a large outbound transfer of about 354MB while the agent storage subsystem was already under IO contention from a concurrent workload. The upload may have stalled, timed out, or been rejected/dropped by the remote endpoint; VerifySrc then exited with code 2 and CloudBees classified the step as Aborted.'}]

 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_systemic_verifysrc_upload_abort', 'name': 'Probable systemic VerifySrc upload abort: remote endpoint failure or script timeout', 'description': 'The RCA identifies the probable root cause as a systemic VerifySrc upload operation issue.  Root Causes:  [{'id': 'rc_farm_wide_kerberos_ticket_expiry', 'name': 'Farm-wide Kerberos ticket expiry causing QCDFS CIFS authentication failure', 'description': "Expired Kerberos credentials prevented CIFS/SMB session setup to \\\\QCDFS.qualcomm.com, causing source files on /mnt/qcdfs/ to be inaccessible during VerifySrc. Log symptoms:  [{'id': 'symptom_exit_code_2', 'name': 'VerifySrc exit code 2', 'description': 'CloudBees failedStep.exitCode was 2. The RCA interprets this as a script-level abort or failed utility return code.'}, {'id': 'symptom_aborted_reason', 'name': 'CloudBees failed reason Aborted', 'description': "CloudBees executionContext.failedReason was 'Aborted', indicating the step was terminated before natural completion."}, {'id': 'symptom_io_await_spike', 'name': 'IO await spike to 35.7ms', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.io.await peaked at approximately 35.7ms around 06:19:42Z, shortly after VerifySrc started.'}, {'id': 'symptom_io_util_26', 'name': 'IO utilization reached 26%', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.io.util reached 26% around the same time as the IO await spike.'}, {'id': 'symptom_network_burst_inbound', 'name': 'Large inbound network burst', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.net.bytes_rcvd showed a peak of approximately 257MB received in a single interval around 06:19:16–06:19:30Z, consistent with a large source fetch.'}, {'id': 'symptom_network_burst_outbound', 'name': 'Large outbound network burst', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed approximately 144MB sent later in the window, described as possibly verification results or artifact metadata upload.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_user_drop', 'name': 'Abrupt CPU user drop', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.cpu.user dropped from approximately 2.7% to approximately 0.1% near the end of the step window, consistent with process termination.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_iowait', 'name': 'CPU iowait rose to 0.93%', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.cpu.iowait peaked at approximately 0.93%, indicating processes blocked on IO during the step window.'}, {'id': 'symptom_system_load_2_04', 'name': 'System load rose to 2.04', 'description': 'System load rose to 2.04 during the VerifySrc step and declined after the abort, consistent with IO-blocked activity according to the RCA.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_storage_io_contention', 'name': 'Probable storage IO contention during large source fetch', 'description': 'Probable root cause: VerifySrc initiated a large source package download of approximately 257MB. As downloaded data was written to local storage on san-w175-ja4-6-06, IO await spiked to 35.7ms and IO utilization reached 26%. The RCA concludes this storage IO contention likely caused the fetch or verification operation to stall or exceed a timeout threshold, leading to abort and exit code 2. Confidence stated as medium-high overall, with high confidence for IO contention and abort, but only medium confidence on the exact abort trigger.'}]

Step logs or agent scheduling data are required.'}, {'id': 'gap_no_monitors_configured', 'name': 'No Datadog monitors configured for agent host', 'description': 'The RCA states no monitors were configured for san-w175-ja4-6-06 during the execution window.'}]

 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_outbound_transfer_timeout_under_io_pressure', 'name': 'Probable outbound transfer timeout or abort under pre-existing IO pressure', 'description': 'The report identifies the probable root cause as the VerifySrc step initiating a large outbound transfer of about 354MB while the agent storage subsystem was already under IO contention from a concurrent workload. 'symptom_disk_usage_jump_secondary', 'name': 'Disk usage jump after this job', 'description': "Datadog metrics show disk usage on san-w175-ha1-4-07 was 5.13% at this job's step time and 42.1% during The RCA treats this as a secondary risk, not the cause of this job's failure."}, {'id': 'symptom_overlapping_job_execution', 'name': 'Overlapping execution on same agent', 'description': 'Job f179cb72 reportedly started on san-w175-ha1-4-07 at 06:16:52Z while this job was still in teardown and before this job ended at 06:18:16Z.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_systemic_verifysrc_upload_abort', 'name': 'Probable systemic VerifySrc upload abort: remote endpoint failure or script timeout', 'description': 'The RCA identifies the probable root cause as a systemic VerifySrc upload operation issue. A sustained outbound transfer of approximately 74MB began before VerifySrc formally started and continued during the step, after which VerifySrc aborted with exit code 2. The same pattern across multiple WLAN TFH jobs and two agents points to either an unavailable or slow remote upload endpoint, or a VerifySrc script timeout that is too short for the data volume. The exact trigger is not confirmed without step logs.'}]

Log symptoms:  [{'id': 'symptom_exit_code_2', 'name': 'VerifySrc exit code 2', 'description': 'CloudBees failedStep.exitCode was 2. The RCA interprets this as a script-level abort or failed utility return code.'}, {'id': 'symptom_aborted_reason', 'name': 'CloudBees failed reason Aborted', 'description': "CloudBees executionContext.failedReason was 'Aborted', indicating the step was terminated before natural completion."}, {'id': 'symptom_io_await_spike', 'name': 'IO await spike to 35.7ms', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.io.await peaked at approximately 35.7ms around 06:19:42Z, shortly after VerifySrc started.'}, {'id': 'symptom_io_util_26', 'name': 'IO utilization reached 26%', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.io.util reached 26% around the same time as the IO await spike.'}, {'id': 'symptom_network_burst_inbound', 'name': 'Large inbound network burst', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.net.bytes_rcvd showed a peak of approximately 257MB received in a single interval around 06:19:16–06:19:30Z, consistent with a large source fetch.'}, {'id': 'symptom_network_burst_outbound', 'name': 'Large outbound network burst', 'description': 'Datadog metrics showed approximately 144MB sent later in the window, described as possibly verification results or artifact metadata upload.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_user_drop', 'name': 'Abrupt CPU user drop', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.cpu.user dropped from approximately 2.7% to approximately 0.1% near the end of the step window, consistent with process termination.'}, {'id': 'symptom_cpu_iowait', 'name': 'CPU iowait rose to 0.93%', 'description': 'Datadog metric system.cpu.iowait peaked at approximately 0.93%, indicating processes blocked on IO during the step window.'}, {'id': 'symptom_system_load_2_04', 'name': 'System load rose to 2.04', 'description': 'System load rose to 2.04 during the VerifySrc step and declined after the abort, consistent with IO-blocked activity according to the RCA.'}]
    
 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_storage_io_contention', 'name': 'Probable storage IO contention during large source fetch', 'description': 'Probable root cause: VerifySrc initiated a large source package download of approximately 257MB. As downloaded data was written to local storage on san-w175-ja4-6-06, IO await spiked to 35.7ms and IO utilization reached 26%. The RCA concludes this storage IO contention likely caused the fetch or verification operation to stall or exceed a timeout threshold, leading to abort and exit code 2. Confidence stated as medium-high overall, with high confidence for IO contention and abort, but only medium confidence on the exact abort trigger.'}]

 Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_outbound_transfer_timeout_under_io_pressure', 'name': 'Probable outbound transfer timeout or abort under pre-existing IO pressure', 'description': 'The report identifies the probable root cause as the VerifySrc step initiating a large outbound transfer of about 354MB while the agent storage subsystem was already under IO contention from a concurrent workload. The upload may have stalled, timed out, or been rejected/dropped by the remote endpoint; VerifySrc then exited with code 2 and CloudBees classified the step as Aborted.'}]
    
 Data Gaps:  [{'id': 'gap_no_datadog_log_pipeline', 'name': 'No application-level logs forwarded to Datadog', 'description': 'The Datadog Agent was installed and reporting metrics, but no log pipeline was configured for san-w175-ha1-4-07, resulting in zero log entries from the host.'}, {'id': 'gap_step_stdout_stderr_missing', 'name': 'VerifySrc stdout/stderr not available in supplied evidence', 'description': 'The report states that step-level stdout/stderr must be retrieved directly from CloudBees to confirm the exact abort trigger.'}, {'id': 'gap_unknown_outbound_destination', 'name': 'Destination of outbound transfer unknown', 'description': 'The remote endpoint receiving the approximately 354MB outbound transfer could not be identified from available metadata.  Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_farm_wide_Kerberos_ticket_expiry_QCDFS_auth_failure', 'name': 'Farm-wide Kerberos ticket expiry causing QCDFS CIFS authentication failure', 'description': 'The VerifySrc step failed because the build agent could not authenticate to \\\\QCDFS.qualcomm.com over CIFS/SMB to access source files.  Root Causes:  [{'id': 'root_cause_storage_io_contention', 'name': 'Probable storage IO contention during large source fetch', 'description': 'Probable root cause: VerifySrc initiated a large source package download of approximately 257MB. As downloaded data was written to local storage on san-w175-ja4-6-06, IO await spiked to 35.7ms and IO utilization reached 26%. The RCA concludes this storage IO contention likely caused the fetch or verification operation to stall or exceed a timeout threshold, leading to abort and exit code 2. Confidence stated as medium-high overall, with high confidence for IO contention and abort, but only medium confidence on the exact abort trigger.'}]
